finding.summary: The track is too hot: true peak is -0.3 dBFS, dangerously close to clipping, while the average level is low (integrated -20 LUFS, crest factor 12 dB). The +5 dB track fader is the culprit.
finding.probable_cause: The track volume is set to +5 dB, pushing a dynamic source with a 12 dB crest factor so that its true peaks hit -0.3 dBFS. No compression or limiting is in the chain to control the dynamics before the fader.
finding.confidence_reason: Capture isolation is verified, silence fraction is low (0.05), and all key metrics are measured. The track has no FX, so the fader directly explains the peak level.

evidence_refs.description: +5 dB, the current fader gain applied to the track

proposal.reason: Lower the track fader to provide safe headroom. The current +5 dB boost causes true peaks at -0.3 dBFS, risking clipping. Reducing to 0 dB gives about 5.3 dB headroom, which is safer while a compressor can be added later to raise average loudness without hitting 0 dBFS.
